title: Rust: AddrMode addr_len() uses static tuple instead of match - correctness depends on enum discriminant order
description:
In python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:38-44, AddrMode.addr_len property uses tuple indexing:

    _ADDR_LEN_TABLE: tuple[int, ...] = (0, 2, 8, 0)  # indexed by AddrMode value

The enum values are explicitly defined as NONE=0, SHORT=1, EXTENDED=2, ELIDED=3, so the tuple indices match correctly.

However, there's no compile-time or test-time assertion ensuring this remains true. If someone adds a new AddrMode variant or changes the values, the lookup table may become incorrect.

Location: python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:38-45
Risk: Low - values are stable spec-defined constants.
Fix: Consider adding a test that asserts len(_ADDR_LEN_TABLE) >= max(AddrMode) + 1.
close_reason: Same as project-LICHEN-1cz5: added test coverage to verify lookup table correctness
